feature: VISION.md at repo root + README mental-model update
description: Create VISION.md at repo root that documents the four operating modes (autopilot, careful, diagnose, postmortem) with concrete usage examples for each. Update README.md to reference VISION.md in its mental-model section and to surface the new commands (incident, approve, rollback, audit, playbook, postmortem).

successCriteria.description: VISION.md exists at /Users/bober4ik/agent-bober/VISION.md, >=200 lines. Sections in order: 'Why agent-bober Exists', 'The Four Modes', 'Mode 1: Autopilot', 'Mode 2: Careful-Flow', 'Mode 3: Diagnose (Incident Response)', 'Mode 4: Postmortem', 'Choosing a Mode', 'Configuration Reference', 'The Foundation: Behavior-Shaping Discipline'.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: test -f VISION.md && wc -l (>=200); grep each section heading.

successCriteria.description: Each mode section documents: (a) when to use, (b) command/skill entry point, (c) what gets created on disk, (d) a concrete worked example with 5-10 line shell session. Examples must be REAL — copy-pasteable to a clean agent-bober checkout.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read each section. Verify all four sub-sections per mode. Spot-check example commands match Sprint 14/24 CLI surface.

successCriteria.description: 'Choosing a Mode' section has a decision tree or table guiding the user: 'building a feature in a spike? → autopilot'; 'changing production behavior? → careful-flow'; 'production system is broken? → diagnose'; 'post-incident retrospective? → postmortem'.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read section. Verify decision tree/table exists.

successCriteria.description: 'Configuration Reference' section documents every new config field added across this spec (pipeline.mode, pipeline.checkpointMechanism, pipeline.checkpointOverrides, pipeline.maxCheckpointIterations, pipeline.approvalTimeoutMs, pipeline.prPollMs, pipeline.allowAutopilotRiskyActions, observability.providers, incident.autoPostmortem, incident.playbookAutoInvokeThreshold, telemetry.enabled — Sprint 28 adds the last one).
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Grep VISION.md for each config field name. Verify each has documentation.

successCriteria.description: 'The Foundation' section explains the behavior-shaping discipline (Iron Laws, Red Flags, Rationalization-Prevention, anti-pattern catalog, HARD-GATEs) and credits obra/superpowers as source.

README.md updated: existing content preserved, new 'Operating Modes' section near the top links to VISION.md, new 'Commands' subsection (or table addition) lists the new CLI surface (incident start/status/end/list/abort, approve, reject, list-approvals, rollback, audit show, playbook list/show/search, postmortem generate/show).
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read README.md diff. Verify the new section exists and lists each new command.

successCriteria.description: Internal cross-links: AGENTS.md (Sprint 6) gains a link to VISION.md. COMMANDS.md (existing) gains entries for all new commands.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Grep AGENTS.md and COMMANDS.md for VISION.md and each new command name.

expectedChanges.description: Top-level vision document explaining the four modes
Add Operating Modes section + Commands subsection
Add entries for new CLI subcommands

generatorNotes:
VISION.md is the artifact someone reads to understand 'what is this?' in <10 minutes. Optimize for that.

Opening hook (the 'Why agent-bober Exists' section):
```
agent-bober is a software engineering teammate. It operates in four modes — autopilot for spikes, careful-flow for production changes, diagnose for incidents, postmortem for retrospectives. The foundation across all four modes is behavior-shaping discipline ported verbatim from obra/superpowers: Iron Laws, Red Flags, Rationalization-Prevention tables. The harness coordinates many agents; the disciplines keep each one rigorous.
```

Worked example for each mode (concrete shell):

Mode 1 (Autopilot):
```bash
$ bober plan 'Add CSV export to the users table page'
$ bober run
# 4 sprints execute, each evaluated, committed; ~30 minutes
```

Mode 2 (Careful-flow):
```bash
$ bober config set pipeline.mode careful
$ bober plan 'Migrate from JWT to session cookies'
$ bober run --checkpoint=disk
# At each checkpoint, .bober/approvals/<id>.pending.json appears
$ bober list-approvals
$ bober approve post-research-spec-20260524-jwt-1
# After reviewing the spec
$ bober approve post-plan-spec-20260524-jwt --edit ./my-edits.md
# Pipeline resumes with edits applied
```

Mode 3 (Diagnose):
```bash
$ bober incident start '500 errors on checkout endpoint' --severity S2
# Incident inc-20260524-500-errors-on created
# bober-diagnoser starts investigating
$ bober incident status inc-20260524-500-errors-on
# Phase: investigating
# Latest diagnosis (confidence: high): connection pool exhausted...
# Risky action proposed: kubectl scale ...
$ bober approve risky-action-act-1  # the gate fired
# Deployer executes; postcondition passes
# Resolution criteria: api.checkout.error_rate < 0.001 for 10m
# Verifying... resolved.
# Postmortem auto-generated at .bober/incidents/.../postmortem.md
```

Mode 4 (Postmortem):
```bash
$ bober postmortem show inc-20260524-500-errors-on
# Renders the synthesized postmortem
$ bober postmortem generate inc-20260524-500-errors-on  # regenerate after artifact additions
```

Decision table (Choosing a Mode):
| Situation | Mode |
|-----------|------|
| Building a feature in a spike, no prod impact | Autopilot |
| Changing production behavior, want diff approval | Careful-Flow |
| Production is broken right now | Diagnose |
| After resolving an incident, want a retrospective | Postmortem |
| Refactoring code with high test coverage | Autopilot |
| Refactoring code that touches user-facing behavior | Careful-Flow |

Configuration reference table: alphabetical by field name with default, since-sprint, description.

The Foundation section: brief (~20 lines) explanation of what Iron Laws/Red Flags/Rationalization-Prevention DO at runtime, with one concrete example from agent-bober's actual prompts. Credit obra/superpowers with MIT attribution.
evaluatorNotes:
Verify every worked example is REAL — try to execute the command syntax (dry-run if needed) against the post-Tier-3 agent-bober and confirm the commands exist. An example referencing `bober approve` is wrong if the actual CLI subcommand is `bober approvals approve` (or whatever Sprint 9 actually wired).

Verify the Configuration Reference covers EVERY new field. Cross-check by listing all schema fields added across Sprints 9, 10, 12, 14, 16, 20, 23, 28. Missing fields in the reference is a documentation regression.

Verify the 'Choosing a Mode' decision table covers the obvious cases AND the gray-area cases. A user with 'refactoring code with high test coverage' should map clearly to a mode, even if it's the same as another row.

Verify cross-links are bidirectional where it makes sense: VISION.md → AGENTS.md, AGENTS.md → VISION.md, README.md → both. COMMANDS.md → VISION.md is one-way; VISION.md mentions commands inline.

Readability check: open VISION.md fresh, time how long it takes to understand the four modes. Target: <10 minutes. If reading the doc and still confused, structure isn't working.

Verify no fabrication: VISION.md must not claim features the spec didn't deliver. If Sprint 22 deferred percent-of-baseline metric comparison, VISION.md must not claim it works.
